reasoning: We do not have a search tool available, so we must rely on outside view and the market estimate. The market estimate on 2025-10-30 was 11%. From Nov 9 to Dec 31, there are approximately 35 trading days left in the year. A 5% single-day drop in the S&P 500 is historically rare, occurring roughly 0.1%-0.15% of trading days over the long run, though they tend to cluster during highly volatile market regimes. If we were in a normal volatility regime, the probability over 35 days would be < 5%. The market's 11% estimate suggests an elevated level of volatility or concern, but as another week and a half has passed without resolution, the probability should have slightly decayed from 11%. Therefore, I estimate the probability at 10%.
